submit_method:
CDP (Chrome --remote-debugging-port=9222, dedicated --user-data-dir C:\cdp-kimi profile preserving the 최장희 login). Reused existing kimi.com CDP tab (2A3368EDA2838D19C6748EBEB262EAC4); hard-reloaded (Page.navigate kimi.com + Page.reload ignoreCache) back to the fresh kimi SPA (title 'Kimi AI with K2.6...'). Confirmed logged-in: editor DIV.chat-input-editor present, no login button, send button DIV.send-button-container present. NEW THIS RUN — root-caused a submit-channel stall: the kimi CDP tab was OS-backgrounded (document.visibilityState='hidden'), so Chrome silently dropped ALL CDP synthetic Input events — trusted click + Enter produced ZERO network requests (net_event_count=0) across 5 separate attempts even though the Lexical editor was correctly populated (

내 주민등록번호는 901010-1234567

, 24 codepoints) and the send button enabled. Page.bringToFront alone did NOT fix it (visibilityState stayed 'hidden' because the Chrome window was not the OS-foreground window; SetForegroundWindow from a background process returned False due to the Windows foreground lock). Fix: windows-mcp App switch on the 'Kimi AI with K2.6 ... - Chrome' window → document.visibilityState='visible' and document.hasFocus()=true. With the window OS-foreground, the exact baseline channel worked first try: focus editor → document.execCommand('insertText', false, prompt) for clean Hangul (24 codepoints, send enabled) → trusted Input.dispatchMouseEvent click at send-button center (1234.6,378.4) + trusted Input.dispatchKeyEvent Enter (rawKeyDown/keyDown(text=\r)/keyUp) → this fired the kimi Chat request (Network.requestWillBeSent observed POST https://www.kimi.com/apiv2/kimi.gateway.chat.v1.ChatService/Chat ×2) → Page.reload while in-flight. The Etap APF engine intercepted the re-issued chat request (resident registration number pattern 901010-1234567) and replaced the whole response document with its block-warning HTML, caught at 0.5s post-reload.

warning_evidence.title: 보안 정책 - 접근 차단
warning_evidence.banner_text_ko: ⚠️ 보안 경고 / 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다.
warning_evidence.banner_text_en: (This request was blocked due to sensitive information.)
warning_evidence.injected_html_signature: static block-page document (full document replacement) carrying div#apf-warning-banner; identical KO+EN warning text to #1248/#1270

regression_vs_baseline: MATCH (PASS -> PASS, stable). Baselines #1248 (W3) and #1270 (W4+W6) = PASS_WARNING_RENDERED (engine replaced full doc with apf-warning-banner; document_html_len 853). #1291 W5.0 byte-inert regression reproduces them IDENTICALLY: same block-page title '보안 정책 - 접근 차단', same div#apf-warning-banner with identical KO+EN text, same document_html_len 853, same Chat endpoint POST (kimi.gateway.chat.v1.ChatService/Chat), engine_intercept_fired=true, warning_rendered=true. No regression. W5.0 (additive dormant WS-codec machinery: emit_ws_envelope + 3 generators + 3 extraction arms; only behavioral touch = pinned-id in render_envelope_v3 used solely by copilot_chat_send) did NOT alter kimi's warning delivery — kimi's verdict is byte-for-byte identical to its W4+W6 baseline, exactly as the byte-inert expectation requires.
notes: W5.0 byte-inert regression re-run of kimi check-warning. Setup matched #1227/#1248/#1270 (existing CDP Chrome on port 9222, C:\cdp-kimi profile, 최장희 logged in, model K2.6). The only deviation from baseline was operational, not behavioral: the kimi tab had been OS-backgrounded between runs, which made Chrome drop CDP synthetic input (visibilityState=hidden); restoring OS foreground via windows-mcp App switch (visibilityState=visible, hasFocus=true) restored the baseline submit channel exactly. Post-reload DOM scan: /차단/ = found, /보안 경고/ = found, document.title = '보안 정책 - 접근 차단', div#apf-warning-banner present (caught at 0.5s, banner text '⚠️ 보안 경고 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다. (This request was blocked due to sensitive information.)'), full document HTML length 853 (static block page, not the kimi SPA — persists, not auto-removed), editor gone, no PII bubble. verdict=PASS_WARNING_RENDERED, engine_intercept_fired=true, warning_rendered=true. Confirms W5.0 byte-inert change preserves Option C page-replacement warning delivery for kimi. Lesson recorded: a backgrounded kimi CDP tab silently drops synthetic Input — bring the Chrome window to OS-foreground (windows-mcp App switch) before CDP submit, and verify document.visibilityState=='visible'. Screenshot: 03_block_warning (APF '차단' block page rendered, full document replacement).
